world.title: An Incredible Share Of Migrant Households Use Welfare, Study Finds
world.description: Nearly half of households in the United States headed by a non-citizen benefit from some form of traditional government welfare, according to a newly published analysis. Forty-seven percent of migrant households are taking advantage of one or more traditional welfare programs, according to a study released by the Center for Immigration Studies (CIS), a group that advocates for lower levels of immigration.

world.title: AMECEA: Youth Invited for 21st AMECEA Plenary, Urged to Prepare for Active Engagement
world.description:
Dalphina Rubyema
Young people from across the Association of Member Episcopal Conferences in Eastern Africa (AMECEA) region have been invited to participate in the 21st AMECEA Plenary Assembly, scheduled to take place in Nairobi, Kenya, from July 19 to 27 this year. They have also been urged to prepare themselves in order to ensure active participation.
The invitation was extended by the AMECEA Secretary General, Fr. Anthony Makunde, during the closing of a training workshop for lay leaders within the Church. The workshop was held at the Tanzania Episcopal Conference (TEC) and brought together various groups of lay leaders, including youth, women, and men from member countries of the association.
The workshop also involved heads of pastoral departments from AMECEA countries, leaders of apostolic movements, lay councils, and AMECEA officials. Various topics were presented, emphasizing the importance of involving lay people in different Church plans and strategies, guided especially by the Final Document of the Synod of Bishops, which highlights communion, participation, and mission.
Regarding the AMECEA Plenary Assembly, Fr. Makunde urged the youth selected by their respective countries to ensure active participation, noting that they are an essential part of the Church and that the theme of the assembly focuses on young people.
“We will send invitations to each country. You, the youth, are an integral part of this Assembly, and you must prepare yourselves to share your ideas. Invitations and guidelines will be sent to your respective conferences,” he said.
In addition to the youth, all participants in the four-day workshop were encouraged to be good ambassadors in implementing the recommendations of the Final Document of the Synod of Bishops. They were reminded that all baptized members of the Church are called to journey together in the same boat, without leaving any group behind.
During the workshop, participants grouped as women leaders, men, youth, and clergy presented their recommendations. Among other proposals, the youth called on the Church to recognize the importance of involving them in its plans, to listen to and act on their suggestions, to develop programs that respond to their needs especially those that allow them to explore and innovate, to provide in-depth catechesis for young people, and for Church institutions to prioritize employment opportunities for qualified youth in various fields. They also emphasized the importance of accompaniment and urged chaplains to give greater attention to young people.
Women leaders, for their part, recommended that the Church listen to them and value their professional expertise by making appropriate use of their skills in advancing the Church. They also called for full inclusion, with chaplains dedicating time, thought, and wisdom to listening to them. Additionally, they proposed the development of a manual to guide various matters, including clarifying their roles and boundaries in Church leadership.
Male leaders recommended stronger collaboration between lay leaders and priests, the establishment of a coordination platform at the AMECEA level, and greater integration between male and female leaders.
Fr. Makunde stated that all the recommendations have been received and will be worked on at various levels before being presented to the AMECEA bishops for implementation guidelines.
“Even as we consider your recommendations and prepare for the AMECEA Plenary Assembly, be aware that we are also preparing questionnaires that will be sent to your conferences. These will be completed as contributions from each country, and once compiled, they will be discussed during the General Assembly,” he said.
